events.title: Israel launches strikes in southern Lebanon
events.desc: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 14 people, including civilians and paramedics, amidst ongoing hostilities. This occurred despite Iran's earlier threats to retaliate if such attacks continued.

events.title: US helicopter crashes near Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: A US Apache gunship helicopter crashed near the Strait of Hormuz. The two pilots were reportedly rescued and are reported to be unharmed. The cause of the crash remains under investigation.

events.title: Israel launches strikes on Iran
events.desc: Israel launched renewed strikes on Iran, reportedly in defiance of warnings from US President Donald Trump. This action is seen as an attempt by Israel to assert its influence in upcoming peace negotiations.

events.title: Global spending on nuclear weapons surges
events.desc: Global spending on nuclear weapons reached a record high of $119 billion in 2025, a 19% increase from the previous year, according to a report by the International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons. The US leads this surge in expenditure.

events.title: Trump warns Netanyahu against new Iran war
events.desc: US President Donald Trump reportedly warned Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu that Israel could find itself isolated if it initiated another war with Iran, highlighting a potential rift in US-Israel strategy.

events.title: Lebanese president appeals for talks, not war
events.desc: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun directly appealed to the Israeli government and its people to pursue negotiations rather than military action, stating that a military solution would not bring security.

events.title: Iran top negotiator threatens to defeat US blockade
events.desc: Iran's top negotiator, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, threatened to overcome the US naval blockade, stating that Iran would fight and negotiate on its own terms, with the ultimate goal of ending the war and achieving stable security.

events.title: US military aided Israel in intercepting Iran's missiles
events.desc: The US military reportedly assisted Israel in intercepting Iranian missile attacks. This intervention occurred despite public opposition from the White House towards Israeli strikes near Beirut.

events.title: Iran and Israel pause strikes but warn of retaliation
events.desc: Both Iran and Israel have indicated a pause in their strikes, with Israel stating it is holding fire "at the moment." Iran's armed forces also confirmed they had ceased military action, though both sides warned of retaliation if the ceasefire is breached.

events.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Gaza City
events.desc: Israeli airstrikes on four residential buildings in Gaza City resulted in the deaths of 11 people, including women and children. The Israeli military stated that four Hamas security officials were killed in the attacks.

events.title: Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
events.desc: Iranian drone strikes targeted Kuwait's airport, causing one death and dozens of injuries. Iran stated this attack was retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and an island.

events.title: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf
events.desc: The US military conducted strikes against Iranian drones and radar sites in the Persian Gulf. In response, Iran claimed to have targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, testing the fragile ceasefire.

events.title: Lebanese general killed in Israeli attack
events.desc: An Israeli attack in south Lebanon resulted in the deaths of three soldiers, including a Lebanese general. The Israeli military is investigating the incident amidst ongoing clashes with Hezbollah.

events.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburb
events.desc: Israel conducted strikes on a suburb of Beirut, which it stated were in response to Hezbollah firing at Israeli territory. This occurred days after a US-brokered truce.

events.title: Iran's President Xi and North Korea's Kim pledge deeper cooperation
events.desc: Chinese President Xi Jinping is on a rare visit to North Korea, where he has committed to strengthening cooperation with Kim Jong Un. The summit signals a deepening of ties between the two nations.
